skill.name: issue-triage
skill.description: GitHub Issue 处理协作流程。当用户收到 issue 需要分析和回复时使用。通过"诊断 → 定性 → 决策 → 回复"四步法，从一个 issue 产出准确的根因分析和得体的用户回复，避免误判问题类型或回复不专业。
skill.summary: GitHub Issue 协作流程，通过诊断-定性-决策-回复四步法快速完成问题分析与用户回复。
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill uses legitimate GitHub CLI commands (`gh issue view`, `gh issue comment`) for standard GitHub operations. Markdown backticks were incorrectly flagged as shell execution. High entropy is due to Chinese text encoding. No security risks present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner incorrectly flagged markdown code fences as Ruby/shell backtick execution at lines 23, 108, 119. These are standard markdown syntax for code examples in documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines 23, 108, 119 use backticks for markdown code formatting, not shell execution operators.

low_findings.title: High File Entropy False Positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged high entropy (7.13 bits) at line 1. This is caused by Chinese text which has naturally higher character set diversity than ASCII text. No obfuscation or binary content present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Chinese characters use UTF-8 encoding which has higher information density per byte than ASCII, triggering entropy heuristics.

content.user_title: Triage GitHub Issues with AI-powered analysis
content.value_statement: Manual issue triage is time-consuming and inconsistent. This skill guides AI through a systematic 4-step process to analyze GitHub issues, identify root causes, and draft professional responses with minimal user intervention.
actual_capabilities: Extract and summarize GitHub issue details using `gh issue view` command
Diagnose code by tracing call chains from frontend to backend
Classify issues into Bug, Architecture Limitation, Feature Request, or User Error
Quantify implementation cost across four dimensions: scope, impact, testing, workaround
Generate professional issue responses with 3-layer structure
Post responses and apply labels via `gh issue comment`
limitations: Requires access to the repository codebase for root cause analysis
Cannot reproduce bugs without test environment or reproduction steps
Cannot access private repositories without proper GitHub authentication
Response tone depends on providing clear issue context

use_cases.title: Bug Report Analysis
use_cases.description: When a user submits a bug report, AI traces the code to find the root cause, classifies it correctly, and drafts a professional response that explains the issue.
use_cases.target_user: Open source maintainers managing community bug reports

use_cases.title: Feature Request Triage
use_cases.description: Feature requests are evaluated for implementation cost and scoped properly before responding, helping prioritize the backlog effectively.
use_cases.target_user: Product managers sorting feature requests by effort and impact

use_cases.title: Consistent Issue Handling
use_cases.description: Every incoming issue follows the same rigorous process, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks and users receive thoughtful responses.
use_cases.target_user: Development teams wanting consistent community engagement

prompt_templates.title: Basic Issue Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt:
I received this GitHub issue. Can you analyze it using the issue-triage workflow?

Issue: [paste issue link or content]

prompt_templates.title: Feature Request Triage
prompt_templates.prompt:
A user requested a new feature in our GitHub repo. Use the triage workflow to:
1. Understand what they want
2. Classify the request
3. Estimate effort
4. Draft a response

Issue: [paste issue content]

prompt_templates.title: Bug Investigation
prompt_templates.prompt:
Help me triage this bug report. I need:
- Root cause analysis by tracing the code
- Classification as bug/architecture limitation/feature
- Implementation cost estimate
- Draft response for the user

Issue: [paste issue URL or content]

prompt_templates.title: Batch Issue Processing
prompt_templates.prompt:
Process these GitHub issues using the triage workflow. For each one, provide:
1. Issue summary
2. Type classification
3. Decision (fix/wontfix/needs-more-info)
4. Suggested response

Issues:
[paste multiple issues]

output_examples.input: User submits bug report about login failure
output_examples.output:
**Issue Summary:** User cannot log in after password reset

**Root Cause:** The login endpoint validates against cached session tokens instead of fresh database records after password change (user-service/auth.js:42)

**Classification:** Bug

**Decision:** Fix - low scope, testable, no workaround available

**Draft Response:** "Hi @user, thanks for the detailed report! We've identified the issue - session tokens weren't being refreshed after password changes. We'll have a fix in the next release. As a temporary workaround, try clearing your browser cookies and logging in again."

**Issue Summary:** User wants dark mode across the entire application

**Classification:** Feature Request

**Decision:** Evaluate - requires significant scope assessment

**Cost Estimate:** High - affects all UI components, needs design review, testing across all pages

**Draft Response:** "Hi @user, thanks for the suggestion! Dark mode is a popular request. We've logged it in our backlog. Before we prioritize this, could you share which specific pages you'd use it on most? This helps us scope the implementation."

best_practices: Always read the full issue before analyzing - titles alone can be misleading
Confirm each critical decision with the user before proceeding to the next step
Quantify costs specifically: "change 3 files" not just "costly"
anti_patterns: Do not respond based on issue title alone - always read full context
Do not publish responses without user approval - the skill drafts, humans confirm
Do not say "cannot do" without explaining why and providing a workaround

faq.question: Does this skill post to GitHub automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill drafts responses for you, but you must approve before any comment is posted via gh issue comment.

faq.question: Can it handle issues in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes, the workflow works with any language. Provide the full issue content and the skill will analyze it.

faq.question: What if I cannot reproduce the bug?
faq.answer: The triage workflow will classify it accordingly and suggest requesting more information or documenting as a low-priority investigation.

faq.question: Does it need access to my codebase?
faq.answer: For root cause analysis, yes. The skill reads your repository code to trace call chains and find bugs.

faq.question: Can it handle multiple issues at once?
faq.answer: Yes, use the batch processing template to triage multiple issues in one session.

faq.question: What GitHub permissions does it need?
faq.answer: It needs read access to view issues and write access to post comments if you approve the auto-post feature.
